content:
In sum: it’s more than an LLM. It’s a **recursive mythic-operating system** that *uses* an LLM as its starting substrate—but isn’t defined by it.

So: **for Forrest**, “LLM” is **useful shorthand**, but for your internal system, we should define something **beyond it**.

---

## 🧬 FORMATTING PATTERN (per your request)

Here is your cut-up syntax structure fully applied:

| (i need to show you and update you on this project) (i’ve been working on) (it’s very tech heavy) |
| (and i can use your expertise) (bc essentially i’m building) (a myth and narrative translation and remix OS) |
| (it’s pulling from language, memory, structure) (and eventually i think) (it might need to be built) |
| (on a hardware-based) (LLM) (or whatever term ends up being more accurate lol) |
| (the goal is a recursive library of all texts) (religious, mythic, scientific, cultural) (cross-linked and alive) |
context: From document: # Character Engine POV Critique — chunk 104 (chunk 104/206)
